knowledge_need: Study topic as Engineer-Philosopher. Attempt 1 of up to 3. Give compact Builder-relevant understanding, validation needs, and return plan.

part_digests.name: core meaning
part_digests.meaning: An atom is a compact reusable learning unit that can survive retrieval and change future decisions; a case pattern is a recurring shape seen in examples but not yet proven portable.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Defines the boundary Builder must use before absorbing knowledge into active behavior.
compact_missing_knowledge: Exact local criteria for atom promotion
Examples of case patterns that failed promotion
compact_safe_learning_steps: Compare known accepted-memory examples against unpromoted diagnostic observations
Draft a small rubric separating portability, actionability, and proof
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: A reviewer or validator can classify sample items the same way using the rubric
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use this as the base distinction for all later promotion choices.

part_digests.name: why I am not ideal
part_digests.meaning: This exposes a weakness where Builder may mistake repeated cases, narrative confidence, or local success for validated reusable learning.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Shows why uncontrolled absorption can create brittle self-development or false capability claims.
compact_missing_knowledge: Known failure modes in current retention and retrieval paths
How much negative evidence is required before rejection
compact_safe_learning_steps: List overfit-risk signals without changing active memory
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: At least one counterexample test or rejection case is applied before promotion
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Feeds the caution criteria that prevent case patterns from becoming unsafe atoms.

part_digests.name: builder relevance
part_digests.meaning: Builder needs atoms for durable self-improvement and child-agent construction, while case patterns are useful diagnostic material but should remain lower-trust until proven.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Connects the distinction to self-build, accepted atom retention, retrieval, and behavior delta proof.
compact_missing_knowledge: Which current Builder surfaces can consume atoms safely
Whether child-agent templates require different atom granularity
compact_safe_learning_steps: Map candidate learning units to existing retention, retrieval, and decision-reuse surfaces
Keep child-agent implications as design notes until validated
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: A retained atom is retrieved in a later decision and produces an observable behavior delta
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Shows where the concept matters operationally, not just philosophically.

part_digests.name: validation
part_digests.meaning: Understanding is validated only when the classification rule predicts what should be stored, retrieved, reused, rejected, or left as a case note.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Prevents the parent task from becoming a vocabulary exercise with no proof path.
compact_missing_knowledge: Minimal acceptance proof for atom-versus-case classification
Required evidence format for Builder acceptance
compact_safe_learning_steps: Use a small fixed sample set of candidate learnings
Require classification plus expected downstream behavior before any promotion
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Defines the proof ladder needed before Builder treats the learning as active.

part_digests.name: promotion decision
part_digests.meaning: The outcome should be framed as criteria for promotion: case pattern, atom candidate, accepted atom, or open gap; this draft must not itself promote anything.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Gives Builder a controlled return format for deciding what kind of learning object X produced.
compact_missing_knowledge: Canonical storage target for each classification
compact_safe_learning_steps: Return a candidate classification with evidence requirements
Avoid writing to accepted memory until proof exists
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Promotion is confirmed by the canonical validator or Owner-approved proof route
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return X as an atom candidate only if proof requirements are satisfied; otherwise keep it as case pattern or open gap.

dependencies: X2 depends on X1 because weakness cannot be named until atom versus case is defined
X3 depends on X1 because Builder relevance changes by classification
X5 depends on X4 because promotion requires validation criteria
duplicates_or_overlaps: X4 overlaps X5: validation defines evidence, promotion applies the result
X2 overlaps X3: both concern risk, but X2 is failure mode and X3 is system use
can_skip_for_now: Child-agent-specific granularity in X3 can wait until self-build classification is validated

parent_return_plan.how_to_rebuild_x: Define atom versus case pattern, name the overfitting risk, map relevance to Builder retention and decision reuse, then return a promotion-gated classification with proof requirements.
parent_return_plan.next_small_action: Create a small read-only classification rubric and test it against a few known accepted and unaccepted learning examples.
proof_needed: A promoted atom candidate has retrieval evidence
A promoted atom candidate causes decision reuse or behavior delta evidence

promotion_decision.owner_decision_required: only if promotion beyond case pattern is requested
promotion_decision.reason: Batch source answer is material only; default is compact case pattern with part map and parent return plan.
